title: A Musical
content:
Outside the rain upon the street,
    The sky all grim of hue,
    Inside, the music-painful sweet,
    And yet I heard but you.

    As is a thrilling violin,
    So is your voice to me,
    And still above the other strains,
    It sang in ecstasy.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Laurence Dunbar, titled "A Musical", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
